summary: Introduction to spreadsheet concepts, working with LibreOffice Calc, cell addressing, data entry, formatting, manipulation, formulas, functions, charts, sorting, filtering, and freezing panes.

topics.notes: Steps to create a new spreadsheet document in LibreOffice Calc or similar applications, including launching the application and opening a blank workbook.

topics.topic: Concept of Cell Address, Row and Column, and Selecting a Cell
topics.notes: A spreadsheet is organized into rows (numbered) and columns (lettered). Each cell is identified by a unique cell address combining column letter and row number (e.g., A1, B3). Cells can be selected individually or as a range.
Entering different types of data into spreadsheet cells including text (labels), numbers, and dates. Data entry is confirmed with Enter or Tab key.
Configuring spreadsheet print settings such as paper size, orientation (portrait/landscape), margins, and print area before printing a sheet.
Sending a spreadsheet to a printer with options to print the active sheet, selected cells, or the entire workbook, including setting print range and scaling.
Saving a spreadsheet using Ctrl+S in its native format (.ods or .xlsx) or exporting it to other formats such as PDF or CSV.
Opening an existing spreadsheet file using Ctrl+O or the File menu, and closing the current document using Ctrl+W or File > Close.
Editing data already entered in a cell by clicking the cell and modifying content in the formula bar, or double-clicking the cell to edit inline.
Applying text formatting to cells including font type, size, color, bold, italic, underline, and setting horizontal or vertical alignment of content within cells.

topics.topic: Cut, Copy, Paste and Paste Special
topics.notes: Moving (Cut: Ctrl+X) or duplicating (Copy: Ctrl+C) cell content and pasting it (Ctrl+V) to another location. Paste Special (Ctrl+Shift+V) allows selective pasting of values, formats, or formulas only.
Adjusting the row height and column width by dragging borders or using the Format menu to set precise measurements for better data display.

topics.topic: Inserting and Deleting Rows and Columns
topics.notes: Adding new rows or columns at a specified position, or removing existing ones to reorganize spreadsheet data using the Insert and Delete options.
Using the AutoFill handle (small square at cell corner) to automatically extend a series of data such as numbers, dates, days, or months across adjacent cells.
Sorting rearranges data in ascending or descending order based on one or more columns. Filtering displays only rows that meet specified criteria, hiding others temporarily.
Locking specific rows or columns (e.g., headers) so they remain visible while scrolling through large spreadsheets using the View > Freeze Rows and Columns option.

topics.topic: Using Formulas for Numbers (Addition, Subtraction, Multiplication and Division)
topics.notes: Writing arithmetic formulas in cells starting with '=' to perform calculations: addition (+), subtraction (-), multiplication (*), and division (/).
A quick tool (Alt+=) that automatically inserts a SUM formula to add up a range of adjacent cells in a row or column.
Built-in spreadsheet functions: SUM adds a range of values, COUNT counts numeric entries, MAX returns the largest value, MIN returns the smallest value, and AVERAGE calculates the mean of a range.
Creating visual representations of spreadsheet data using chart types: Bar charts compare categories, Pie charts show proportions of a whole, and Line charts display trends over time.
